name: Classic Mexican Flan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt the sugar over moderate heat, stirring frequently, until it becomes a dark, caramel-colored liquid. Remove from heat and pour into a 4-inch metal ring mold or 8 individual molds. Quickly turn the mold to coat the bottom and sides with the caramel. Let cool so that the caramel hardens.
2. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C). In an electric blender, combine the sweetened condensed milk, milk, eggs, egg yolks, almond extract, and vanilla extract. Blend until well mixed.
3. Pour the mixture into the mold. Place the mold in a larger pan filled with water to a depth of about 1/2 inch. Bake for 1 hour.
4. Remove from the oven and let cool. Then refrigerate up to 2 days. Cover the mold with an inverted serving platter.
5. Hold the mold and platter together, turn them over, and lift off the mold. The caramel will fall as a liquid sauce over the custard.
